text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver lived high up in an ancient oak tree talking to squirrels. They loved listening. One day Oliver heard the birds gossiping that butterflies loved honeysuckle. Oliver nibbled on some tender vines. They fluttered. Oliver flew away with butterflies.

There was a garden fair. Oliver saw ladybugs chasing sunbeams. They stopped. Oliver fluttered by. Bugs ate flypins.

Back home, Oliver dreamed of butterflies carrying flags. They brought springtime. He sewed an emblem of four petals. Each morning he flew out bearing it. Friends gathered.

Years later, flowers bloomed. Children traced Oliver's trail.

Oliver slept. He dreamed old trees spoke. They told of heroes who vanished. But the hero stayed.

Long after, kids made their own hero tales. Oliver dreamt they'd save wildlife. But heroism was everywhere.

Now days: butterflies decorate garden ponds.

End.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|>
